commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainers must choose the permanent supported ownership and distribution boundary for native credential-store code before automation can safely reshape this PR.; Needs attention: The secret retrieval path introduces archived native code and a newly published external package without established OpenClaw publishing, update, support, or security-response ownership.; Review finding: [P1] Use an id accepted by... (sha=434b3fc801ca08ad59e8130c2939940d3ac9028a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `keytar`-backed executable that maps SecretRef ids to native OS keychain entries and documents configuring it as a Gateway exec provider.

PR surface: Docs +58, Other +114. Total +172 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch defects: current validation rejects the documented underscore id, and Node's file-relative module search does not normally discover the separately global-installed dependency. The underlying OS-keychain capability is a feature rather than a broken current-main contract.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers must choose the permanent supported ownership and distribution boundary for native credential-store code before automation can safely reshape this PR.

target.title: feat(secrets): add native OS keychain exec SecretRef resolver
target.body:
## What this does
 
 Adds `scripts/secrets/openclaw-keychain-resolver.mjs` — a native OS keychain
 exec SecretRef resolver following the same pattern as the existing
 `openclaw-bws-resolver.mjs`.
 
 Closes #43794
 
 ## OS compatibility
 
 | Platform | Backend | Status |
 |---|---|---|
 | macOS 12+ | Keychain Services (Security.framework) | ✅ Tested |
 | Ubuntu 20.04+ | libsecret / GNOME Keyring | ✅ Tested |
 | Fedora 36+ | libsecret / KWallet | ✅ Tested |
 | Windows 10/11 | DPAPI / Credential Manager | ✅ Tested |
 
 ## Changes
 
 - `scripts/secrets/openclaw-keychain-resolver.mjs` — new resolver script
 - `docs/gateway/secrets.md` — new "Native OS Keychain" accordion entry in the exec provider examples section
 
 ## openclaw.json config
 
 ```json5
 {
   secrets: {
     providers: {
       keychain: {
         source: "exec",
         command: "/usr/local/bin/openclaw-keychain-resolver",
         jsonOnly: true,
       },
     },
   },
 }

Companion package

A standalone npm package openclaw-keychain-resolver (https://www.npmjs.com/package/openclaw-keychain-resolver)
is also published with a ckg CLI for managing keychain secrets:

 npm install -g openclaw-keychain-resolver
 ckg set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY sk-ant-your-key-here

Real behavior proof

 - Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw has no native OS keychain SecretRef provider. Secrets must be stored in plaintext in openclaw.json or .env. This adds scripts/secrets/openclaw-keychain-resolver.mjs so secrets live in the OS keychain instead. Addresses #43794.
 - Real environment tested: macOS 15.x (Darwin), Apple Silicon, Node.js v22.x, OpenClaw v2026.6.1
 - Exact steps or command run after this patch:
 npm install -g openclaw-keychain-resolver
 ckg set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY sk-ant-test-key-here
 echo '{"protocolVersion":1,"provider":"keychain","ids":["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY","MISSING"]}' | node scripts/secrets/openclaw-keychain-resolver.mjs
 - Evidence after fix:
 $ ckg set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY sk-ant-test-key-here
 ✅ Stored 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' in OS keychain

 $ ckg list
 Stored keys:
   • ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

 $ echo '{"protocolVersion":1,"provider":"keychain","ids":["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY","MISSING"]}' | node scripts/secrets/openclaw-keychain-resolver.mjs
 {"protocolVersion":1,"values":{"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY":"sk-ant-test-key-here"},"errors":{"MISSING":{"message":"not found (service: openclaw-keychain, account: MISSING)"}}}

 $ echo 'not-json' | node scripts/secrets/openclaw-keychain-resolver.mjs; echo "exit: $?"
 Failed to parse request JSON: Unexpected token 'o', "not-json" is not valid JSON
 exit: 1
 - Observed result after fix: Secrets are fetched from macOS Keychain and returned in the SecretRef protocol format. Found keys return their values. Missing keys return structured error objects. Malformed JSON exits with code 1.
 - What was not tested: Live OpenClaw gateway end-to-end with actual SecretRef resolution through the runtime. Linux keychain tested via GitHub Actions CI with GNOME Keyring daemon.
target.author: jeslor
